browserNameSearchTab: Ice Cover Duration, Europe, Yearly,  20m/100m, (2016-present), V1
browserSearchPath: CLMS Bio-geophysical Parameters, Water Bodies, Water and Ice Cover
collectionDescription:
The **Ice Cover Duration (ICD)** product describes how long inland waters are covered by ice. For each pixel and for a given hydrological year, it provides the number of days with ice cover. The hydrological year starts on 1 September. It is based on ice cover maps derived from **Sentinel-2** optical data (Water/Ice Cover WIC S2) and **Sentinel-1** C-band Synthetic Aperture Radar data (Water/Ice Cover WIC S1). 
  
  The product is generated at the European scale with a spatial resolution of **20 m × 20 m**. 
    
  **Data availability**: from 2016 to the present. The data can be downloaded in multiple projections and pixel spacings. 

  More information [here](https://land.copernicus.eu/en/products/water-bodies/high-resolution-ice-cover-duration).

layers.longDescription: Number of days of ice cover on water during the hydrological year.

layers.shortDescription: Quality layer for the ice cover duration (ICD) layer
layers.longDescription: Quality layer providing a basic assessment of the ice cover duration layer.

layers.shortDescription: Number of days with Sentinel-1 based observations during the hydrological year
layers.longDescription: Number of days with Sentinel-1 based observations used in the interpolation of the ice cover time series within the hydrological year.

layers.shortDescription: Number of days with Sentinel-2 based observations during the hydrological year
layers.longDescription: Number of days with Sentinel-2 based observations used in the interpolation of the ice cover time series within the hydrological year.
